body_txt: It's musical theatre law. The frequently good-looking , almost always lovestruck, and without a doubt naive, yet good-hearted young man central to the story must sing with lyrical, boyish grace. In other words: he's a tenor. In a large portion of opera (particularly Verdi and Puccini), The Protagonist is a tenor no matter what his age or personality. Compare the Innocent Soprano , his Distaff Counterpart and probable love interest. Contrast Evil Sounds Deep .
